problemSolved: Project management platforms with 40-60 APIs are complex to navigate, requiring users to learn custom UIs and manually click through workflows.
features: Exposed 15+ REST API endpoints as structured Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools.
Enabled LLM agents to dynamically resolve user intent to correct API calls.
Structured prompt schemas for tool parameters, usage intent, and response contracts.
architecture: A FastMCP server hosting project management APIs, orchestrated by an AgentSDK-based agent executing multi-step workflows.
myContribution: Designed and deployed the FastMCP server, structured the prompt schemas, and delivered the production chatbot interface.
challenges: Agent hallucination in mapping unstructured inputs to correct API schemas.
learnings: Authoring rigid schema boundaries and strict context contracts is critical for stable tool-calling agents.

problemSolved: Flat vector embeddings lose structural and dependency relationships in complex codebases or internal documentation.
features: Dual data backends combining vector search and knowledge graph relationships.
3-agent orchestration pipeline: Decider, Data Retriever, Response Generator.
Intent-based routing to prevent retrieval ambiguity and hallucinations.
architecture: ChromaDB vector store handles raw textual documentation search, while Neo4j maps codebase file relationships, linked via an intent classifier routing queries appropriately.
myContribution: Architected the hybrid retrieval system and implemented the 3-agent orchestration pipeline.
challenges: Integrating vector search results with graph traversals cleanly to synthetically feed the generator agent.
learnings: Graph databases capture structural context (like code imports) that flat embeddings completely miss.

problemSolved: Analyzing hundreds of legacy property documents for legal links and ownership transfers is manually slow and error-prone.
features: Async processing pipeline handling 500+ legacy files using Redis task workers.
Parallel dual workers: one for embedding ingestion, one for Neo4j relationship mapping.
Configurable relationship-extraction rules to identify legal and ownership associations.
architecture: Documents uploaded via FastAPI are queued in Redis. Dual workers pull jobs to embed text in vector stores and build a relationship graph in Neo4j in parallel.
myContribution: Built the background Redis queue worker framework and integrated Neo4j graph construction.
challenges: Managing parallel sync locks and database write conflicts in Neo4j during concurrent ingestions.
learnings: Separating ingestion workers by database target prevents bottlenecks and speeds up parsing jobs by 2x.

problemSolved: Recruiters spend hours manually scanning hundreds of resumes, leading to screening bottlenecks.
features: Automated resume parser and candidate profile screening.
Semantic similarity scoring against Job Descriptions using NVIDIA NIM embeddings.
FastAPI backend serving concurrent ranking calculations in real-time.
architecture: FastAPI receives PDF resumes, extracts text, generates embeddings via NVIDIA NIM, and scores similarity against a job description vector.
myContribution: Developed semantic similarity matching backend and integrated NVIDIA NIM APIs.
challenges: Optimizing API latency for concurrent high-volume resume scans.
learnings: Caching model outputs and batched embedding generation drastically lowers processing costs and response time.

problemSolved: Tracking monthly contributions, payments, and defaulters in small friend-circle chit funds is tedious when managed via Excel/paper.
features: Secure role-based access for admins and regular fund members.
QR code-based payment registration and verification.
Defaulter list generation and contribution tracking dashboard.
architecture: React frontend communicates with a Node.js/Express server connecting to MongoDB for storing transactions and user roles.
myContribution: Developed the complete backend service, MongoDB schemas, and payment approval workflow.
challenges: Securing admin-only routes to ensure transaction records couldn't be manipulated by regular members.
learnings: JWT-based role authentication is lightweight and highly effective for micro-financial management.

problemSolved: Providing fair salary offers based on subjective interview scores and candidate profiles without statistical backing.
features: Data-driven estimation model using Linear Regression.
Features analyzed: experience, test scores, and interview performance metrics.
Quick and lightweight prediction engine suitable for HR portals.
architecture: A linear regression model trained on history sheets, predicting target salaries based on experience years and score inputs.
myContribution: Preprocessed raw dataset, trained regression models, and evaluated feature weights using Scikit-learn.
challenges: Managing multicollinearity between interview scores and general years of experience.
learnings: Feature scaling and checking coefficients help keep predictions grounded and explainable to HR managers.
